data.title: Write SC13 performance benchmark test: 1,000-ticket hierarchy link operations under 5s
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## What
Add `test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets()` to `tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py`.

## Implementation Steps

1. Import: use the existing `graph` pytest fixture (importlib-loaded ticket-graph module) and `_write_ticket` helper.
2. Build 10 epics, each with 10 stories (parent_id=epic), each with 10 tasks (parent_id=story) = 1,000 tickets total.
   - Use ticket_type=`epic` / `story` / `task` appropriately.
   - Assign unique IDs: `epic-{i:02d}`, `story-{i:02d}-{j:02d}`, `task-{i:02d}-{j:02d}-{k:02d}`.
3. Perform 10 cross-story `add_dependency()` calls: link task-00-00-00 → task-01-00-00, task-00-01-00 → task-01-01-00, ... (tasks in different stories).
4. Wrap the 10 `add_dependency()` calls in `time.monotonic()` and assert elapsed < 5.0s.
5. After timing, assert that at least one story-level dep was promoted (e.g., `build_dep_graph('story-00-00', str(tracker_dir))['deps']` is non-empty) — ensures hierarchy enforcement actually ran, not just a no-op.

## TDD Requirement
Test function name: `test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets`
Given: a realistic 10×10×10 ticket hierarchy (epic→story→task) in tmp_path
When: 10 cross-story `add_dependency()` calls with `relation='depends_on'`
Then: all 10 calls complete in <5.0 seconds total AND at least one story-level dep was written (proving hierarchy enforcement ran)

This test is RED before `resolve_hierarchy_link()` (3f65-65ee) and `add_dependency()` integration (983e-7fff) are implemented. After implementation, timing must be <5s.

## File to Modify
`tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py` — add the new test function at the end of the file.

## .test-index Update
Add RED marker to .test-index:
```
plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py: tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py [test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py -x -q` exits non-zero for the new test function before hierarchy enforcement is implemented (RED state)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py::test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets -x 2>&1; test $? -ne 0
- [ ] `ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py
- [ ] `ruff format --check tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff format --check tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py
- [ ] Test function `test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets` exists in test_ticket_graph.py
  Verify: grep -q 'def test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets' tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py
- [ ] Test asserts elapsed < 5.0 seconds
  Verify: grep -q 'elapsed < 5' tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py
- [ ] Test builds realistic hierarchy with epic/story/task ticket_type values
  Verify: grep -q 'ticket_type.*epic\|ticket_type.*story\|ticket_type.*task' tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py
- [ ] .test-index has RED marker for test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets
  Verify: grep -q '\[test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_1000_tickets\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] Test uses module-imported `add_dependency()` directly (not subprocess)
  Verify: grep -q 'graph.add_dependency\|add_dependency.*tracker_dir' tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py
